Project Lost Piglet 2¶

Investigating Human Threat Actors via Cognitive Friction & Machine Learning

Author: Justin McCormick
Affiliation: Penn State Berks — B.S. Cybersecurity Analytics & Operations
Role: Solo Researcher
Sensor Deployment: Cowrie SSH honeypot · GCP us-east4 · Feb 11 – Mar 5, 2026 (23-day soak)
Notebook Version: 1.0 · April 2026

Pipeline Orientation¶

This notebook documents the end-to-end ML pipeline for Project Lost Piglet 2 (LP2) — an independent cyber-intelligence study testing whether telemetry from a high-interaction SSH honeypot can be machine-scored at scale to separate human threat actors from commodity botnet automation. The hypothesis: deliberately engineered Cognitive Friction (password-protected archives, psychologically loaded filenames, non-standard logic puzzles) forces measurable decision latency that becomes the anchoring feature for behavioral classification.

The pipeline ingests raw Cowrie JSONL from a 23-day deployment (174,963 events), enriches it with geographic and SSH-client metadata, runs a three-model ensemble (Isolation Forest + HDBSCAN + Decision Tree surrogate for explainability) under EVT/KS dynamic thresholding, and terminates in an audience-specific Threat Intelligence Report. Each phase is self-contained and can be reviewed independently.

Phase Map¶

Phase Module Purpose
I Data Ingestion & Preprocessing Merge LP1 + LP2 archives into a unified session DataFrame
II Enrichment & Normalization ip-api geolocation, ASN resolution, column normalization
III-A Classification — Isolation Forest Session-level anomaly scoring with EVT/KS dynamic thresholding
III-B Density Clustering — HDBSCAN Behavioral taxonomy; ejects botnet mass as noise (-1)
III-C Explainability — Decision Tree Surrogate Human-readable rules behind the ensemble outputs
IV Behavioral Assessment MITRE tactic mapping, malware campaign analysis, LP1↔LP2 comparison
V Keystroke & Burst Analysis Execution velocity across taxonomy groups (human vs. bot timing signatures)
VI HASSH Fingerprinting SSH client identification and cross-campaign attribution
VII TTP Mapping (MITRE ATT&CK) Two-stage semantic classifier — TF-IDF cosine + keyword hierarchy
VIII Session Forensics & Cognitive Friction Dwell-time forensics on the highest-value interactive session
IX Cross-Session Attribution Skeleton-based clustering; LP1→LP2 infrastructure continuity
X Threat Intelligence Report SOC Manager tactical brief + Strategic Intelligence product

Headline Findings¶

  • 174,963 events → 6 human-interactive sessions — greater than 99.96% noise reduction
  • RedTail cryptominer campaign captured end-to-end — 6 unique payloads across 49 delivery cycles, originating from 130.12.180.51 (UK)
  • LP1 → LP2 cross-node attribution — 33-day persistent botnet infrastructure identified across two independent deployments
  • Top threat origins: China (56%), India (15.8%), Malaysia (6.6%)
In [1]:

PHASE I: DATA INGESTION & PREPROCESSING¶

In [2]:
# @title
# ==========================================
# PHASE I: DATA INGESTION & PREPROCESSING
# ==========================================
# Sources:
#   LP1 — CowrieLogs/LP1/cowrie.log*  (plain text, 4-day baseline soak, no persona)
#   LP2 — CowrieLogs/LP2/cowrie.json* (JSON Lines, 23-day Jim Harris persona soak)
#
# Outputs consumed by all downstream phases:
#   df                 — master telemetry DataFrame (LP1 + LP2, schema-unified)
#   cmd_df             — command input events with per-command lure flags
#   session_sequences  — session-level command fingerprints + lure aggregates
#   upload_df          — file upload events (malware delivery)
#   download_df        — file download events
#   malware_samples_df — unique malware samples by SHA hash
#   top_creds          — top credential pairs with lure cross-reference
#   daily_lp1/lp2      — daily event volumes for engagement lifecycle analysis

import json
import logging
import re
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ─── PATHS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA_ROOT = Path(r'C:\Users\sascr\OneDrive\Desktop\ClaudeProjects\LP2\CowrieLogs')
LP1_DIR   = DATA_ROOT / 'LP1'
LP2_DIR   = DATA_ROOT / 'LP2'
logger.info('DATA_ROOT: %s  (exists: %s)', DATA_ROOT, DATA_ROOT.exists())

# ─── OPERATIONAL CONSTANTS (Jim Harris persona — LP2 only) ─────────────────────

LURE_FILE_KEYWORDS: List[str] = [
    'beachhouse', 'bucketlist', 'corporate_quarter', 'crypto_wallet',
    'exwife', 'honey_due', 'ihatemyboss', 'jim_carrey', 'mypassword',
    'november_check', 'passwords', 'tic_tac_toe',
]

DEEP_TRAP_KEYWORDS: List[str] = [
    'crypto_wallet', 'exwife', 'passwords', 'mypassword',
]

# Short numeric passwords matched as whole tokens to prevent false positives
# (e.g. '12345' appearing as a substring of '12345678' in botnet nohup payloads).
LURE_PASSWORDS_WORD_BOUNDARY: List[str] = ['12345']
LURE_PASSWORDS_SUBSTRING: List[str] = [
    'BeachHouse2025!', 'hunter2',    'Spring2024!',  'Welcome!23',
    'Password1',       'Root@123',   'AppSvc2023',   'dbadmin2022',
    'Admin!234',       'cisco123',   'routerpass',   'OfficeWifi!',
    'gh_pass_2022',    'AwsAdmin123','docker123',    'Helpdesk1',
    'monitor123',      'VpnAccess!', 'Pass1234',     'paypalpw', 'stream123',
]
LURE_PASSWORDS: List[str] = LURE_PASSWORDS_WORD_BOUNDARY + LURE_PASSWORDS_SUBSTRING

_LURE_WB_PATTERN = re.compile(
    '|'.join(r'\b' + re.escape(pw) + r'\b' for pw in LURE_PASSWORDS_WORD_BOUNDARY),
    re.IGNORECASE,
) if LURE_PASSWORDS_WORD_BOUNDARY else None


def _is_lure_password_hit(command_lower: str) -> bool:
    """Word-boundary check for short numeric passwords; substring for the rest."""
    if _LURE_WB_PATTERN and _LURE_WB_PATTERN.search(command_lower):
        return True
    return any(pw.lower() in command_lower for pw in LURE_PASSWORDS_SUBSTRING)


# ─── PHASE I-A: LP1 PLAIN LOG PARSER ───────────────────────────────────────────

_LP1_LINE    = re.compile(r'^(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}T[\d:.]+Z)\s+\[([^\]]+)\]\s+(.*)')
_LP1_CONNECT = re.compile(r'New connection: ([\d.]+):(\d+) \(([\d.]+):(\d+)\) \[session: ([a-f0-9]+)\]')
_LP1_LOGIN   = re.compile(r"login attempt \[b\'([^\']+)\'/b\'([^\']*)\'] (failed|succeeded)")
_LP1_HASSH   = re.compile(r'SSH client hassh fingerprint: ([a-f0-9]+)')
_LP1_VERSION = re.compile(r'Remote SSH version: (.+)')
_LP1_CMD     = re.compile(r'^CMD: (.+)')
_LP1_CLOSE   = re.compile(r'Connection lost after ([\d.]+) seconds')
_LP1_COMP    = re.compile(r'HoneyPotSSHTransport,(\d+),([\d.]+)')


def parse_lp1_logs(lp1_dir: Path) -> List[dict]:
    """
    Parses Cowrie plain-text logs into normalized event dicts matching LP2 schema.

    Session tracking strategy:
      'New connection' lines carry session + src_ip but no connection ID.
      All subsequent lines carry [HoneyPotSSHTransport,{conn_id},{src_ip}] in
      the component. The first time a (conn_id, src_ip) pair is seen it is bound
      to the most recently opened session for that src_ip, establishing the
      lookup used for all remaining events on that connection.
    """
    ip_to_session:   Dict[str, str]   = {}  # src_ip -> active session
    conn_to_session: Dict[tuple, str] = {}  # (conn_id, src_ip) -> session
    events: List[dict] = []

    for log_file in sorted(lp1_dir.glob('cowrie.log*')):
        with log_file.open('r', encoding='utf-8', errors='replace') as f:
            for raw in f:
                m = _LP1_LINE.match(raw.strip())
                if not m:
                    continue
                ts_str, component, message = m.group(1), m.group(2), m.group(3)
                ts = pd.to_datetime(ts_str, utc=True, errors='coerce')
                if pd.isna(ts):
                    continue

                # --- cowrie.session.connect ---
                cm = _LP1_CONNECT.search(message)
                if cm:
                    src_ip, src_port, dst_ip, dst_port, sess = cm.groups()
                    ip_to_session[src_ip] = sess
                    events.append({
                        'eventid': 'cowrie.session.connect',
                        'timestamp': ts, 'session': sess,
                        'src_ip': src_ip, 'src_port': int(src_port),
                        'dst_ip': dst_ip, 'dst_port': int(dst_port),
                        'source': 'LP1',
                    })
                    continue

                # --- Resolve session via (conn_id, src_ip) ---
                transport = _LP1_COMP.search(component)
                if not transport:
                    continue
                conn_id, src_ip = transport.group(1), transport.group(2)
                key = (conn_id, src_ip)
                if key not in conn_to_session:
                    if src_ip not in ip_to_session:
                        continue
                    conn_to_session[key] = ip_to_session[src_ip]
                session = conn_to_session[key]
                base = {'timestamp': ts, 'session': session, 'src_ip': src_ip, 'source': 'LP1'}

                # --- Login ---
                lm = _LP1_LOGIN.search(message)
                if lm:
                    user, pw, result = lm.groups()
                    events.append({**base,
                        'eventid':  'cowrie.login.success' if result == 'succeeded' else 'cowrie.login.failed',
                        'username': user, 'password': pw,
                    })
                    continue

                # --- HASSH fingerprint ---
                hm = _LP1_HASSH.search(message)
                if hm:
                    events.append({**base, 'eventid': 'cowrie.client.kex', 'hassh': hm.group(1)})
                    continue

                # --- SSH client version ---
                vm = _LP1_VERSION.search(message)
                if vm:
                    events.append({**base, 'eventid': 'cowrie.client.version', 'version': vm.group(1)})
                    continue

                # --- Command input (CMD: lines only, not 'Command found/not found') ---
                cmd_m = _LP1_CMD.match(message)
                if cmd_m and cmd_m.group(1).strip():
                    events.append({**base, 'eventid': 'cowrie.command.input',
                                   'input': cmd_m.group(1).strip()})
                    continue

                # --- Session close ---
                cl_m = _LP1_CLOSE.search(message)
                if cl_m:
                    events.append({**base, 'eventid': 'cowrie.session.closed',
                                   'duration': float(cl_m.group(1))})

    logger.info('[LP1] Parsed %d events from %s', len(events), lp1_dir)
    return events


# ─── PHASE I-B: LP2 JSON INGESTER ──────────────────────────────────────────────

def ingest_lp2_json(lp2_dir: Path) -> List[dict]:
    """
    Ingests Cowrie JSON Lines telemetry from LP2 (dated rotations + active log).
    Tags every record with 'source': 'LP2' for downstream source-aware filtering.
    """
    events: List[dict] = []
    error_count = 0

    for pattern in ['cowrie.json.2026-*', 'cowrie.json']:
        for log_file in sorted(lp2_dir.glob(pattern)):
            with log_file.open('r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                for raw in f:
                    raw = raw.strip()
                    if not raw:
                        continue
                    try:
                        rec = json.loads(raw)
                        rec['source'] = 'LP2'
                        events.append(rec)
                    except json.JSONDecodeError:
                        error_count += 1

    if error_count:
        logger.warning('[LP2] Skipped %d malformed JSON lines.', error_count)
    logger.info('[LP2] Ingested %d events.', len(events))
    return events


# ─── PHASE I-C: COMBINED INGESTION ─────────────────────────────────────────────

def ingest_all() -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    Merges LP1 (plain text) and LP2 (JSON) telemetry into one schema-unified
    master DataFrame. Deduplicates at log-rotation boundaries.

    Returns:
        pd.DataFrame: UTC-timestamped master telemetry.
                      Every row carries 'source' = 'LP1' | 'LP2'.
    """
    lp1_events = parse_lp1_logs(LP1_DIR)
    lp2_events = ingest_lp2_json(LP2_DIR)

    all_events = lp1_events + lp2_events
    if not all_events:
        logger.error('No events ingested. DATA_ROOT=%s  exists=%s', DATA_ROOT, DATA_ROOT.exists())
        return pd.DataFrame()

    df = pd.DataFrame(all_events)
    df['timestamp'] = pd.to_datetime(df['timestamp'], errors='coerce', utc=True)
    df = df.dropna(subset=['timestamp'])

    dedup_cols = [c for c in ['session', 'timestamp', 'eventid'] if c in df.columns]
    pre = len(df)
    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=dedup_cols).reset_index(drop=True)
    removed = pre - len(df)
    if removed:
        logger.info('Deduplication removed %d duplicate events.', removed)

    logger.info(
        'Master telemetry: %d total events  (LP1: %d | LP2: %d)',
        len(df), (df['source'] == 'LP1').sum(), (df['source'] == 'LP2').sum(),
    )
    return df


# ─── PHASE I-D: COMMAND STAGING & COGNITIVE FRICTION SEEDING ───────────────────

def stage_command_telemetry(df: pd.DataFrame) -> Tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:
    """
    Isolates command input events, computes inter-keystroke deltas, and seeds
    cognitive friction lure flags for Phase III ML feature engineering.

    Lure flags are analytically meaningful for LP2 only (Jim Harris persona).
    LP1 sessions correctly carry 0s — no lures were deployed on the baseline node.
    """
    cmd_df = (df[df['eventid'] == 'cowrie.command.input']
              .copy()
              .sort_values(['session', 'timestamp'])
              .reset_index(drop=True))

    cmd_df['time_delta']  = cmd_df.groupby('session')['timestamp'].diff().dt.total_seconds()
    cmd_df['input_clean'] = cmd_df['input'].astype(str).str.strip()
    input_lower           = cmd_df['input_clean'].str.lower()

    cmd_df['lure_file_hit']     = input_lower.apply(lambda x: int(any(kw in x for kw in LURE_FILE_KEYWORDS))).astype(np.int8)
    cmd_df['lure_password_hit'] = input_lower.apply(lambda x: int(_is_lure_password_hit(x))).astype(np.int8)
    cmd_df['deep_trap_hit']     = input_lower.apply(lambda x: int(any(kw in x for kw in DEEP_TRAP_KEYWORDS))).astype(np.int8)

    session_sequences = (
        cmd_df.groupby('session')['input_clean']
        .apply(lambda x: ' | '.join(x))
        .reset_index(name='command_sequence')
    )
    lure_agg = cmd_df.groupby('session').agg(
        lure_file_hits     = ('lure_file_hit',     'sum'),
        lure_password_hits = ('lure_password_hit', 'sum'),
        deep_trap_hits     = ('deep_trap_hit',     'sum'),
    ).reset_index()
    session_sequences = session_sequences.merge(lure_agg, on='session', how='left')
    return cmd_df, session_sequences


# ─── PHASE I-E: FILE TRANSFER & MALWARE STAGING ────────────────────────────────

def stage_file_events(df: pd.DataFrame) -> Tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:
    """Stages upload/download events. Normalizes filename column across Cowrie versions."""
    upload_df   = df[df['eventid'] == 'cowrie.session.file_upload'].copy()
    download_df = df[df['eventid'] == 'cowrie.session.file_download'].copy()
    for frame in [upload_df, download_df]:
        if 'filename' not in frame.columns:
            frame['filename'] = frame.get('outfile', pd.Series(dtype=str))
    logger.info('File transfers staged: %d uploads, %d downloads.', len(upload_df), len(download_df))
    return upload_df, download_df


def stage_malware_samples(upload_df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Aggregates uploaded files by SHA hash. Flags recognized malware families."""
    if upload_df.empty:
        return pd.DataFrame()

    def _family(fn: str) -> str:
        fn = str(fn).lower()
        if 'redtail' in fn:               return 'Redtail (Cryptominer — documented C2 framework)'
        if fn in ('clean.sh', 'setup.sh'): return 'Dropper/Cleanup Script'
        if fn.endswith(('.sh', '.bash')):  return 'Shell Script'
        if any(a in fn for a in ['arm', 'i686', 'x86', 'mips', 'ppc']): return 'ELF Binary'
        return 'Unknown'

    return (
        upload_df.groupby('shasum').agg(
            filename       = ('filename',  'first'),
            delivery_count = ('session',   'count'),
            unique_ips     = ('src_ip',    'nunique'),
            source_ips     = ('src_ip',    lambda x: list(x.unique())),
            first_seen     = ('timestamp', 'min'),
            last_seen      = ('timestamp', 'max'),
        )
        .reset_index()
        .sort_values('delivery_count', ascending=False)
        .assign(malware_family=lambda d: d['filename'].apply(_family))
    )


# ─── PHASE I-F: CREDENTIAL HARVEST ─────────────────────────────────────────────

def harvest_credentials(df: pd.DataFrame, top_n: int = 25) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    Top credential pairs across LP1 + LP2 combined.
    Exact-match lure flag is appropriate here — brute-force pairs are atomic
    tokens, not substrings of longer command strings.
    """
    creds = df[df['eventid'].isin(['cowrie.login.success', 'cowrie.login.failed'])].copy()
    top = (
        creds.groupby(['username', 'password']).size()
        .reset_index(name='count')
        .sort_values('count', ascending=False)
        .head(top_n)
        .reset_index(drop=True)
    )
    top['is_lure_credential'] = top['password'].isin(LURE_PASSWORDS)
    return top


# ─── PHASE I-G: ENGAGEMENT LIFECYCLE VISUALIZATION ─────────────────────────────

def compute_daily_volume(df: pd.DataFrame, source: Optional[str] = None) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Daily event counts, optionally filtered to 'LP1' or 'LP2'."""
    data = df[df['source'] == source] if source else df
    if data.empty:
        return pd.DataFrame()
    daily = data.groupby(data['timestamp'].dt.date).size().reset_index(name='event_count')
    daily.columns = ['date', 'event_count']
    daily['date']       = pd.to_datetime(daily['date'])
    daily['day_number'] = range(1, len(daily) + 1)
    daily['rolling_3d'] = daily['event_count'].rolling(3, min_periods=1).mean()
    daily['delta_pct']  = daily['event_count'].pct_change() * 100
    return daily.sort_values('date').reset_index(drop=True)


def plot_engagement_lifecycle(daily_df: pd.DataFrame, title: str) -> None:
    """Two-panel chart: daily event volume (top) and day-over-day delta (bottom)."""
    if daily_df.empty:
        return
    fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(
        2, 1, figsize=(18, 9), sharex=True,
        gridspec_kw={'height_ratios': [3, 1], 'hspace': 0.05},
    )
    mean_vol   = daily_df['event_count'].mean()
    bar_colors = ['crimson' if v > mean_vol * 2 else 'steelblue'
                  for v in daily_df['event_count']]

    ax1.bar(daily_df['day_number'], daily_df['event_count'],
            color=bar_colors, alpha=0.75, width=0.85)
    ax1.plot(daily_df['day_number'], daily_df['rolling_3d'],
             color='darkorange', linewidth=2.5, linestyle='--', label='3-Day Rolling Avg')
    ax1.axhline(mean_vol, color='gray', linestyle=':', linewidth=1.0,
                label=f'Mean ({mean_vol:,.0f})')
    for _, row in daily_df.nlargest(3, 'event_count').iterrows():
        ax1.annotate(
            f"Day {int(row['day_number'])}\n{int(row['event_count']):,}",
            xy=(row['day_number'], row['event_count']), xytext=(0, 10),
            textcoords='offset points', ha='center', fontsize=8,
            color='crimson', fontweight='bold',
        )
    ax1.set_ylabel('Event Count')
    ax1.set_title(title, fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
    ax1.legend(fontsize=9)
    ax1.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.4)
    ax1.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f'{int(x):,}'))

    deltas = daily_df['delta_pct'].fillna(0)
    ax2.bar(daily_df['day_number'], deltas,
            color=['green' if d >= 0 else 'red' for d in deltas], alpha=0.75, width=0.85)
    ax2.axhline(0, color='black', linewidth=0.8)
    ax2.set_ylabel('Delta DoD (%)')
    ax2.set_xlabel('Day of Soak')
    ax2.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.4)
    ax2.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f'{x:+.0f}%'))
    ax2.set_xticks(daily_df['day_number'])
    ax2.set_xticklabels(daily_df['date'].dt.strftime('%b %d'),
                        rotation=45, ha='right', fontsize=8)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


# ─── PHASE I-H: INGESTION SUMMARY ──────────────────────────────────────────────

def print_ingestion_summary(
    df: pd.DataFrame,
    cmd_df: pd.DataFrame,
    top_creds: pd.DataFrame,
    session_sequences: pd.DataFrame,
    malware_samples: pd.DataFrame,
) -> None:
    lp1_ts    = df[df['source'] == 'LP1']['timestamp']
    lp2_ts    = df[df['source'] == 'LP2']['timestamp']
    soak_lp1  = (lp1_ts.max() - lp1_ts.min()).total_seconds() / 3600 if not lp1_ts.empty else 0
    soak_lp2  = (lp2_ts.max() - lp2_ts.min()).total_seconds() / 3600 if not lp2_ts.empty else 0
    lure_sess = int((session_sequences['lure_file_hits']     > 0).sum())
    deep_sess = int((session_sequences['deep_trap_hits']     > 0).sum())
    pw_sess   = int((session_sequences['lure_password_hits'] > 0).sum())
    lure_cred = int(top_creds['is_lure_credential'].sum())

    print('\n' + '=' * 90)
    print('### PHASE I: INGESTION COMPLETE — MASTER TELEMETRY READY ###')
    print('=' * 90)
    print(f"  Total Events:   {len(df):>8,}  "
          f"(LP1: {(df['source']=='LP1').sum():,} | LP2: {(df['source']=='LP2').sum():,})")
    print(f'  LP1 Soak:       {soak_lp1:>6.1f}h ({soak_lp1/24:.1f} days) — Baseline node, no persona')
    print(f'  LP2 Soak:       {soak_lp2:>6.1f}h ({soak_lp2/24:.1f} days) — Jim Harris persona active')
    print('-' * 90)
    print('  EVENT TYPE INVENTORY:')
    for eid, count in df['eventid'].value_counts().items():
        print(f'    {eid:<54} {count:>8,}')
    print('-' * 90)
    print(f'  Commands Staged:   {len(cmd_df):,}')
    print(f'  Sessions Mapped:   {len(session_sequences):,}')
    print(f'  Credential Pairs:  {len(top_creds):,}')
    if not malware_samples.empty:
        print(f'  Malware Samples:   {len(malware_samples):,} unique payloads (by SHA hash)')
        for _, r in malware_samples.iterrows():
            print(f"    [{r['shasum'][:16]}...]  {str(r['filename']):<24} "
                  f"x{r['delivery_count']}  — {r['malware_family']}")
    else:
        print('  Malware Samples:   None (no file_upload events in this window)')
    print('-' * 90)
    print('  LP2 COGNITIVE FRICTION (Seeded for Phase III ML):')
    print(f'    Lure File Interactions:   {lure_sess} sessions referenced Jim Harris persona files')
    print(f'    Deep Trap Activations:    {deep_sess} sessions accessed high-value lures')
    print(f'    Lure Password Executions: {pw_sess} sessions executed lure-derived commands')
    print(f'    Lure Creds in BF Dict:    {lure_cred}  '
          f"({'HUMAN-INFORMED TARGETING DETECTED' if lure_cred > 0 else 'NULL — dictionary attacks are not file-content-informed'})")
    print('=' * 90 + '\n')


# ─── EXECUTION ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

if __name__ == '__main__':

    df = ingest_all()

    if df.empty:
        logger.error('CRITICAL: No telemetry ingested. Verify CowrieLogs/ path.')
    else:
        # Engagement lifecycle — one chart per source for direct LP1 vs LP2 comparison
        daily_lp1 = compute_daily_volume(df, source='LP1')
        daily_lp2 = compute_daily_volume(df, source='LP2')
        plot_engagement_lifecycle(
            daily_lp1,
            'LP1: Baseline Node — 4-Day Fresh IP Engagement Lifecycle',
        )
        plot_engagement_lifecycle(
            daily_lp2,
            'LP2: Jim Harris Persona — 23-Day Engagement Lifecycle\n'
            'Red bars = coordinated botnet sweeps (>2x mean volume)',
        )

        cmd_df, session_sequences = stage_command_telemetry(df)
        upload_df, download_df    = stage_file_events(df)
        malware_samples_df        = stage_malware_samples(upload_df)
        top_creds                 = harvest_credentials(df)

        print_ingestion_summary(
            df, cmd_df, top_creds, session_sequences, malware_samples_df,
        )

    [048e374baac36d8c...]  redtail.i686             x96  — Redtail (Cryptominer — documented C2 framework)
    [3625d06889695359...]  redtail.arm7             x96  — Redtail (Cryptominer — documented C2 framework)
    [59c29436755b0778...]  redtail.x86_64           x96  — Redtail (Cryptominer — documented C2 framework)

PHASE II: ENRICHMENT & NORMALIZATION¶

In [3]:
# @title
# ==========================================
# PHASE II: ENRICHMENT & NORMALIZATION
# ==========================================
# Enriches master telemetry with geographic + infrastructure metadata (ip-api.com),
# then normalizes key columns for consistent downstream consumption.
#
# Enrichment:
#   - Country, ISP, ASN per unique public src_ip (LP1 + LP2 combined)
#   - RFC1918 private IPs labeled explicitly — not sent to API
#   - Local JSON cache persists results — only new IPs hit the API on re-runs
#   - Exponential backoff on HTTP 429 rate-limit responses
#
# Normalization:
#   - Key column dtype enforcement (timestamps, numerics, strings)
#   - Missing value policy: empty strings for categoricals, NaN for numerics
#   - session_duration_s computed from open/close event pairs

import time
import logging
import requests
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ─── CONSTANTS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

IP_API_BATCH_URL = 'http://ip-api.com/batch?fields=query,country,isp,as'
REQUEST_TIMEOUT  = 10
API_SLEEP_DELAY  = 4.5   # conservative buffer — batch endpoint: 45 req/min free tier
CHUNK_SIZE       = 100
MAX_RETRIES      = 3

# Cache sits next to the data so it survives notebook moves
CACHE_FILE = DATA_ROOT / 'ip_metadata_cache.json'

PRIVATE_PREFIXES = (
    '10.', '172.16.', '172.17.', '172.18.', '172.19.',
    '172.20.', '172.21.', '172.22.', '172.23.', '172.24.',
    '172.25.', '172.26.', '172.27.', '172.28.', '172.29.',
    '172.30.', '172.31.', '192.168.', '127.', '169.254.', '::1',
)

# ─── PHASE II-A: CACHE MANAGEMENT ──────────────────────────────────────────────

def load_cache() -> Dict[str, Dict[str, str]]:
    """Returns cached IP metadata, or empty dict on first run."""
    if CACHE_FILE.exists():
        with CACHE_FILE.open('r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            cache = json.load(f)
        logger.info('[CACHE] Loaded %d pre-enriched IPs. New IPs only will hit the API.', len(cache))
        return cache
    logger.info('[CACHE] No cache found — full API enrichment will run.')
    return {}

def save_cache(metadata_map: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]]) -> None:
    """Persists enrichment results for future re-runs."""
    with CACHE_FILE.open('w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        json.dump(metadata_map, f, indent=2)
    logger.info('[CACHE] %d IP records saved to %s', len(metadata_map), CACHE_FILE)

# ─── PHASE II-B: API ENRICHMENT ────────────────────────────────────────────────

def is_private_ip(ip: str) -> bool:
    return any(ip.startswith(p) for p in PRIVATE_PREFIXES)

def fetch_batch_metadata(ips: List[str], session: requests.Session) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, str]]:
    """
    Fetches country/ISP/ASN for a list of public IPs via ip-api.com batch endpoint.
    Chunks requests to 100 IPs/call and applies exponential backoff on rate limits.
    """
    if not ips:
        return {}

    metadata_map: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = {}
    chunks = [ips[i:i + CHUNK_SIZE] for i in range(0, len(ips), CHUNK_SIZE)]
    logger.info('Enriching %d unique IPs across %d API chunk(s)...', len(ips), len(chunks))

    for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
        for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES):
            try:
                resp = session.post(IP_API_BATCH_URL, json=chunk, timeout=REQUEST_TIMEOUT)
                if resp.status_code == 429:
                    wait = API_SLEEP_DELAY * (2 ** attempt)
                    logger.warning('Rate limit on chunk %d/%d — backoff %.1fs.', i+1, len(chunks), wait)
                    time.sleep(wait)
                    continue
                resp.raise_for_status()
                for entry in resp.json():
                    ip = entry.get('query')
                    if ip:
                        metadata_map[ip] = {
                            'country': entry.get('country', 'Unknown'),
                            'isp':     entry.get('isp',     'Unknown'),
                            'asn':     entry.get('as',      'Unknown'),
                        }
                logger.info('Chunk %d/%d complete (%d IPs).', i+1, len(chunks), len(chunk))
                break
            except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
                logger.error('Network error chunk %d/%d (attempt %d): %s', i+1, len(chunks), attempt+1, e)
                time.sleep(API_SLEEP_DELAY)

        if i < len(chunks) - 1:
            time.sleep(API_SLEEP_DELAY)

    return metadata_map


def enrich_telemetry(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    Maps country, ISP, and ASN onto every row of the master DataFrame.

    Private IPs are labeled 'Private/RFC1918' without an API call.
    The 'asn' column is the primary input to Phase VIII Meta-Actor attribution —
    ASN grouping consolidates IPs that rotate across the same infrastructure.
    """
    if df.empty or 'src_ip' not in df.columns:
        logger.error('Enrichment skipped — DataFrame empty or missing src_ip.')
        return df

    all_ips     = df['src_ip'].dropna().unique().tolist()
    private_set = {ip for ip in all_ips if is_private_ip(ip)}
    public_ips  = [ip for ip in all_ips if ip not in private_set]

    cached      = load_cache()
    uncached    = [ip for ip in public_ips if ip not in cached]

    new_meta: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = {}
    if uncached:
        with requests.Session() as s:
            new_meta = fetch_batch_metadata(uncached, s)

    full_meta = {**cached, **new_meta}
    if new_meta:
        save_cache(full_meta)

    _private = {'country': 'Private/RFC1918', 'isp': 'Private/RFC1918', 'asn': 'Private/RFC1918'}
    _failed  = {'country': 'Lookup Failed',   'isp': 'Lookup Failed',   'asn': 'Lookup Failed'}

    def _resolve(ip: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
        if ip in private_set:
            return _private
        return full_meta.get(ip, _failed)

    df['country'] = df['src_ip'].map(lambda x: _resolve(x)['country'])
    df['isp']     = df['src_ip'].map(lambda x: _resolve(x)['isp'])
    df['asn']     = df['src_ip'].map(lambda x: _resolve(x)['asn'])

    return df


# ─── PHASE II-C: NORMALIZATION ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

def normalize_telemetry(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    Enforces consistent column types and fills missing values across the unified
    LP1 + LP2 DataFrame. Downstream phases can rely on these guarantees:

      - 'timestamp'    : UTC-aware datetime (already set in Phase I)
      - 'src_ip'       : str, no NaN
      - 'session'      : str, no NaN
      - 'eventid'      : str, no NaN
      - 'source'       : str, 'LP1' | 'LP2'
      - 'username'     : str, '' where absent
      - 'password'     : str, '' where absent
      - 'input'        : str, '' where absent
      - 'hassh'        : str, '' where absent
      - 'duration'     : float, NaN where absent
      - 'session_duration_s' : float — computed from session open/close pairs
    """
    # String columns — coerce and fill
    for col in ['src_ip', 'session', 'eventid', 'source', 'username',
                'password', 'input', 'hassh', 'country', 'isp', 'asn']:
        if col in df.columns:
            df[col] = df[col].astype(str).str.strip().replace('nan', '')
        else:
            df[col] = ''

    # Numeric columns — coerce, leave NaN for genuinely absent values
    if 'duration' in df.columns:
        df['duration'] = pd.to_numeric(df['duration'], errors='coerce')

    # Session duration — derived from cowrie.session.connect / cowrie.session.closed pairs
    connects = df[df['eventid'] == 'cowrie.session.connect'][['session', 'timestamp']].rename(
        columns={'timestamp': 'connect_time'})
    closes   = df[df['eventid'] == 'cowrie.session.closed'][['session', 'timestamp']].rename(
        columns={'timestamp': 'close_time'})

    if not connects.empty and not closes.empty:
        session_times = connects.merge(closes, on='session', how='inner')
        session_times['session_duration_s'] = (
            session_times['close_time'] - session_times['connect_time']
        ).dt.total_seconds()
        duration_map = session_times.set_index('session')['session_duration_s'].to_dict()
        df['session_duration_s'] = df['session'].map(duration_map)
    else:
        df['session_duration_s'] = float('nan')

    logger.info('Normalization complete. Shape: %s', df.shape)
    return df


# ─── PHASE II-D: ENRICHMENT SUMMARY ────────────────────────────────────────────

def print_enrichment_summary(df: pd.DataFrame) -> None:
    if df.empty or 'country' not in df.columns:
        return

    _exclude = {'Private/RFC1918', 'Lookup Failed', 'Unknown', ''}
    verified = df[~df['country'].isin(_exclude)]
    failed   = df[df['country'].isin({'Lookup Failed', 'Unknown'})]
    private  = df[df['country'] == 'Private/RFC1918']
    coverage = len(verified) / len(df) * 100 if len(df) else 0

    top_asns = (verified.groupby('asn').size().reset_index(name='events')
                .sort_values('events', ascending=False).head(5))
    top_isps = (verified.groupby('isp').size().reset_index(name='events')
                .sort_values('events', ascending=False).head(5))

    print('\n' + '=' * 90)
    print('### PHASE II: ENRICHMENT & NORMALIZATION COMPLETE ###')
    print('=' * 90)
    print(f"  Total Events:        {len(df):,}  (LP1: {(df['source']=='LP1').sum():,} | LP2: {(df['source']=='LP2').sum():,})")
    print(f'  Unique Source IPs:   {df["src_ip"].nunique():,}')
    print(f'  Verified Coverage:   {len(verified):,} events ({coverage:.1f}%)')
    print(f'  Lookup Failed:       {len(failed):,} events')
    print(f'  Private/RFC1918:     {len(private):,} events')
    print(f'  Countries Identified:{verified["country"].nunique():,}')
    print(f'  Unique ASNs Mapped:  {verified["asn"].nunique():,}')
    print('-' * 90)
    print('  TOP 5 ASNs  (Phase VIII Meta-Actor attribution feed):')
    for _, row in top_asns.iterrows():
        print(f"    {row['asn']:<48} {row['events']:>7,} events  ({row['events']/len(df)*100:.1f}%)")
    print('-' * 90)
    print('  TOP 5 ISPs:')
    for _, row in top_isps.iterrows():
        print(f"    {row['isp']:<48} {row['events']:>7,} events  ({row['events']/len(df)*100:.1f}%)")
    if 'session_duration_s' in df.columns:
        valid_dur = df['session_duration_s'].dropna()
        if not valid_dur.empty:
            print('-' * 90)
            print(f'  Session Durations:   mean={valid_dur.mean():.1f}s  '
                  f'median={valid_dur.median():.1f}s  '
                  f'max={valid_dur.max():.1f}s  '
                  f'({len(valid_dur):,} sessions with open/close pairs)')
    print('=' * 90 + '\n')


# ─── EXECUTION ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

if __name__ == '__main__':
    try:
        df = enrich_telemetry(df)
        df = normalize_telemetry(df)
        print_enrichment_summary(df)
    except NameError:
        logger.error("'df' not defined — ensure Phase I executed successfully.")

2026-04-12 02:00:34,880 - INFO - [CACHE] Loaded 2581 pre-enriched IPs. New IPs only will hit the API.

==========================================================================================
### PHASE II: ENRICHMENT & NORMALIZATION COMPLETE ###
==========================================================================================

  Unique Source IPs:   2,581
  Verified Coverage:   174,963 events (100.0%)
  Lookup Failed:       0 events
  Private/RFC1918:     0 events
  Countries Identified:99
  Unique ASNs Mapped:  573
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  TOP 5 ASNs  (Phase VIII Meta-Actor attribution feed):
    AS37963 Hangzhou Alibaba Advertising Co.,Ltd.     73,739 events  (42.1%)
    AS17813 Mahanagar Telephone Nigam Limited         23,741 events  (13.6%)
    AS134768 CHINANET SHAANXI province Cloud Base network  16,871 events  (9.6%)
    AS45102 Alibaba (US) Technology Co., Ltd.         11,930 events  (6.8%)
    AS14061 DigitalOcean, LLC                          4,209 events  (2.4%)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  TOP 5 ISPs:
    Hangzhou Alibaba Advertising Co                   73,514 events  (42.0%)
    Mahanagar Telephone Nigam Limited                 23,741 events  (13.6%)
    CHINANET SHAANXI province Cloud Base network      16,871 events  (9.6%)
    Alibaba (US) Technology Co., Ltd.                 11,366 events  (6.5%)
    DigitalOcean, LLC                                  4,199 events  (2.4%)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Session Durations:   mean=4.9s  median=2.0s  max=320.2s  (173,255 sessions with open/close pairs)
==========================================================================================

PHASE III-A: CLASSIFICATION - ISOLATION FOREST¶

In [4]:
# @title
# ==========================================
# PHASE III-A: CLASSIFICATION — ISOLATION FOREST
# ==========================================
# Session-level anomaly detection using Isolation Forest with dynamic
# Extreme Value Theory (EVT) thresholding via KS Goodness-of-Fit optimisation.
#
# Rather than assuming a fixed contamination rate (standard IF default: 10%),
# this phase applies Peaks-Over-Threshold (POT) EVT to find the threshold where
# a Generalised Pareto Distribution best fits the anomaly score tail — making
# the boundary data-driven rather than assumption-driven.
#
# Inputs:  cmd_df, session_sequences  (Phase I)
# Outputs: features_df  (consumed by Phase III-B, III-C, and all downstream phases)

import logging
import warnings
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.ensemble import IsolationForest
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
from sklearn.preprocessing import MaxAbsScaler, RobustScaler
from scipy.stats import entropy, genpareto, kstest

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore', category=RuntimeWarning)
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ─── CONSTANTS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Commodity botnet command baseline — commands appearing here are 'known/expected'
# and receive low TF-IDF rarity weight. Novel attacker commands not present in this
# corpus receive high weight, correctly elevating their rarity score.
GLOBAL_BENIGN_CORPUS: List[str] = [
    'uname -a', 'uname -s -v -n -r -m', 'uname -s -m', 'cat /proc/cpuinfo',
    "echo -e 'ok'", "echo 'ok'", 'echo SHELL_TEST',
    'wget http://[URL]', 'curl -O http://[URL]', 'tftp -g',
    'cd /tmp', 'cd /var/run', 'rm -rf *', 'chmod 777 *', 'chmod +x',
    'history -c', 'export PATH=/bin:/usr/bin', 'free -m', 'netstat -anp',
    'ps -ef', 'ps aux', 'cat /proc/mounts', 'ifconfig', 'ip addr',
    'ls /proc', 'cat /etc/passwd', 'id', 'whoami', 'w',
    'chattr -ia .ssh', 'mkdir .ssh', 'echo ssh-rsa',
]

# Bulk data exfiltration patterns — tactical escalation signals
BULK_EXFIL_PATTERNS: List[str] = [
    'find /', 'tar -c', 'tar -z', 'grep -r', 'grep -ir',
    'xargs', 'curl -f', 'wget --post', 'scp ', 'rsync ',
]


# ─── PHASE III-A-1: FEATURE ENGINEERING ────────────────────────────────────────

def build_rarity_scores(
    session_groups: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],
    target_col: str,
) -> Dict[str, float]:
    """
    TF-IDF command rarity scores fitted on GLOBAL_BENIGN_CORPUS + all session texts.
    Commands common in the baseline corpus score low; novel commands score high.
    Capped at 500 commands/session to prevent score dilution from swarm sessions.
    """
    if not session_groups:
        return {}

    session_ids  = list(session_groups.keys())
    corpus_texts = [
        ' '.join(g.head(500)[target_col].dropna().astype(str)).lower()
        for g in session_groups.values()
    ]

    vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
        token_pattern=r'(?u)\b\w+\b',
        max_features=5000,
        sublinear_tf=True,
    )
    vectorizer.fit(GLOBAL_BENIGN_CORPUS + corpus_texts)
    rarity_sums = vectorizer.transform(corpus_texts).sum(axis=1).A1
    return dict(zip(session_ids, rarity_sums))


def extract_session_features(
    cmd_df: pd.DataFrame,
    session_sequences: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None,
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    Extracts per-session features across five groups:

      Temporal:           mean_delta, avg_keystroke_speed (alias), entropy_delta,
                          burst_ratio, biological_variance
      Complexity:         command_complexity, unique_commands
      Semantic:           command_rarity_score (TF-IDF)
      Cognitive Friction: lure_file_hits, lure_password_hits, deep_trap_hits
                          (merged from Phase I session_sequences)
      Tactical:           bulk_exfil_score, friction_bypassed
      Source:             source (LP1 | LP2)
    """
    if cmd_df.empty or 'session' not in cmd_df.columns:
        logger.warning('cmd_df empty or missing session column.')
        return pd.DataFrame()

    target_col     = 'input_clean' if 'input_clean' in cmd_df.columns else 'input'
    session_groups = dict(tuple(cmd_df.groupby('session')))
    rarity_scores  = build_rarity_scores(session_groups, target_col)

    session_source = (
        cmd_df.groupby('session')['source'].first()
        if 'source' in cmd_df.columns else pd.Series(dtype=str)
    )

    rows: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    for sid, group in session_groups.items():
        deltas     = group['time_delta'].dropna()
        safe_group = group.head(500)
        text       = ' '.join(safe_group[target_col].dropna().astype(str)).lower()

        mean_d  = float(deltas.mean())  if not deltas.empty else 0.0
        burst_r = float((deltas < 0.5).mean()) if not deltas.empty else 1.0

        if len(deltas) >= 5:
            ent_d        = float(entropy(np.histogram(deltas, bins=10)[0] + 1))
            bio_variance = (float(deltas.max()) + 0.001) / (float(deltas.median()) + 0.001)
        else:
            ent_d        = 0.0
            bio_variance = 1.0

        rows.append({
            'session_id':           sid,
            'source':               session_source.get(sid, 'Unknown'),
            # Temporal
            'mean_delta':           mean_d,
            'avg_keystroke_speed':  mean_d,   # alias for forensic dossier
            'entropy_delta':        ent_d,
            'burst_ratio':          burst_r,
            'biological_variance':  bio_variance,
            # Complexity
            'command_complexity':   float(safe_group[target_col].astype(str).str.len().mean()),
            'unique_commands':      int(safe_group[target_col].nunique()),
            # Semantic
            'command_rarity_score': rarity_scores.get(sid, 0.0),
            # Tactical
            'bulk_exfil_score':     sum(1 for p in BULK_EXFIL_PATTERNS if p in text),
            'friction_bypassed':    int(any(pw.lower() in text for pw in LURE_PASSWORDS)),
        })

    features_df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
    if features_df.empty:
        return features_df

    # ── Merge Phase I cognitive friction features (single source of truth) ──
    if session_sequences is not None and not session_sequences.empty:
        lure_cols = [c for c in
                     ['session', 'lure_file_hits', 'lure_password_hits', 'deep_trap_hits']
                     if c in session_sequences.columns]
        if len(lure_cols) > 1:
            features_df = features_df.merge(
                session_sequences[lure_cols],
                left_on='session_id', right_on='session', how='left',
            ).drop(columns=['session'], errors='ignore')
            for col in ['lure_file_hits', 'lure_password_hits', 'deep_trap_hits']:
                if col in features_df.columns:
                    features_df[col] = features_df[col].fillna(0).astype(np.int8)
            logger.info('Cognitive friction merged: %d session(s) with lure file interactions.',
                        int((features_df.get('lure_file_hits', pd.Series([0])) > 0).sum()))
    else:
        logger.warning('session_sequences not provided — lure features defaulting to 0.')
        for col in ['lure_file_hits', 'lure_password_hits', 'deep_trap_hits']:
            features_df[col] = np.int8(0)

    return features_df


# ─── PHASE III-A-2: ANOMALY DETECTION ──────────────────────────────────────────

def detect_anomalies(
    features_df: pd.DataFrame,
    min_samples: int = 5,
) -> Tuple[pd.DataFrame, float, float, float]:
    """
    Isolation Forest with KS-optimised Peaks-Over-Threshold EVT thresholding.

    Steps:
      1. Scale: RobustScaler for temporal features, MaxAbsScaler for sparse/lure.
      2. Fit Isolation Forest (contamination='auto').
      3. Extract continuous anomaly scores.
      4. Slide threshold across 1st-20th percentiles; fit GPD to each tail.
      5. Select threshold with minimum KS statistic (best GPD fit).
      6. Classify sessions below the 95th percentile of the optimal GPD as anomalous.

    Returns: (enriched DataFrame, anomaly_rate, threshold, optimal_percentile)
    """
    if len(features_df) < min_samples:
        logger.warning('Insufficient data (%d sessions, min %d). Labeling all as low-risk.',
                       len(features_df), min_samples)
        out = features_df.copy()
        out['anomaly_score']   = 1
        out['predicted_label'] = 'Scanner/Low Volume'
        return out, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0

    temporal_cols = [c for c in ['mean_delta', 'entropy_delta', 'burst_ratio',
                                  'biological_variance', 'command_complexity',
                                  'unique_commands'] if c in features_df.columns]
    sparse_cols   = [c for c in ['command_rarity_score', 'lure_file_hits',
                                  'lure_password_hits', 'deep_trap_hits',
                                  'bulk_exfil_score', 'friction_bypassed']
                     if c in features_df.columns]
    exclude       = {'session_id', 'source', 'anomaly_score', 'predicted_label',
                     'cluster_id', 'avg_keystroke_speed'}
    feature_cols  = [c for c in temporal_cols + sparse_cols if c not in exclude]

    preprocessor = ColumnTransformer([
        ('temporal', RobustScaler(),  [c for c in temporal_cols if c in feature_cols]),
        ('sparse',   MaxAbsScaler(), [c for c in sparse_cols   if c in feature_cols]),
    ], remainder='drop')

    X      = preprocessor.fit_transform(features_df[feature_cols])
    iso    = IsolationForest(contamination='auto', random_state=42, n_jobs=-1)
    iso.fit(X)
    scores = iso.decision_function(X)

    best_ks, best_tau, best_params, opt_pct = float('inf'), np.percentile(scores, 5), None, 5.0

    for p in np.arange(1.0, 21.0, 1.0):
        tau    = np.percentile(scores, p)
        peaks  = tau - scores[scores < tau]
        if len(peaks) < 10:
            continue
        params     = genpareto.fit(peaks)
        ks_stat, _ = kstest(peaks, 'genpareto', args=params)
        if ks_stat < best_ks:
            best_ks, best_tau, best_params, opt_pct = ks_stat, tau, params, p

    threshold = (best_tau - genpareto.ppf(0.95, *best_params)) if best_params else best_tau
    labels    = np.where(scores < threshold, -1, 1)
    anom_rate = float(np.sum(labels == -1)) / len(features_df)

    out = features_df.copy()
    out['anomaly_score'] = labels
    return out, anom_rate, threshold, opt_pct


# ─── PHASE III-A-3: SUMMARY ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def print_isolation_summary(
    features_df: pd.DataFrame,
    anomaly_rate: float,
    threshold: float,
    opt_pct: float,
) -> None:
    if 'anomaly_score' not in features_df.columns:
        return

    total     = len(features_df)
    anomalies = int((features_df['anomaly_score'] == -1).sum())
    lure_sess = int((features_df.get('lure_file_hits', pd.Series([0])) > 0).sum())
    deep_sess = int((features_df.get('deep_trap_hits', pd.Series([0])) > 0).sum())
    lure_pct  = lure_sess / total * 100 if total else 0.0

    print('\n' + '=' * 90)
    print('### PHASE III-A: ISOLATION FOREST COMPLETE ###')
    print('=' * 90)
    print(f'  Sessions Processed:  {total:,}')
    print(f'  High-Risk Outliers:  {anomalies:,}  ({anomaly_rate*100:.4f}% of sessions)')
    if 'source' in features_df.columns:
        for src in ['LP1', 'LP2']:
            s = features_df[features_df['source'] == src]
            if not s.empty:
                a = int((s['anomaly_score'] == -1).sum())
                print(f'    {src}: {len(s):,} sessions  |  {a:,} anomalies ({a/len(s)*100:.2f}%)')
    print('-' * 90)
    print('  EVT Thresholding (KS-Optimised POT):')
    print(f'    Optimal tail percentile: {opt_pct:.1f}th')
    print(f'    Dynamic boundary:        score < {threshold:.4f}')
    print('    No fixed contamination assumption — threshold is data-driven.')
    print('-' * 90)
    print('  COGNITIVE FRICTION (LP2 — Jim Harris Persona):')
    print(f'    Lure file interactions:  {lure_sess:,} sessions ({lure_pct:.4f}% of total)')
    print(f'    Deep trap activations:   {deep_sess:,} sessions')
    print()
    print('  NOTE — Low lure feature importance in Phase III-C is expected:')
    print(f'    {lure_sess} lure sessions / {total:,} total = {lure_pct:.4f}% variance.')
    print('    Near-zero variance prevents tree splits. Lure evidence is substantiated')
    print('    in Phase VIII (Session Forensics) and Phase X (TI Report).')
    print('=' * 90 + '\n')


# ─── EXECUTION ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

if __name__ == '__main__':
    try:
        features_df = extract_session_features(cmd_df, session_sequences=session_sequences)
        if not features_df.empty:
            features_df, calc_rate, calc_threshold, opt_p = detect_anomalies(features_df)
            print_isolation_summary(features_df, calc_rate, calc_threshold, opt_p)
        else:
            logger.error('No session features extracted — verify cmd_df from Phase I.')
    except NameError as e:
        logger.error('Missing upstream dependency: %s — ensure Phases I & II ran.', e)

PHASE III-B: DENSITY-BASED CLUSTERING (HDBSCAN)¶

In [5]:
# @title
# ==========================================
# PHASE III-B: DENSITY-BASED CLUSTERING (HDBSCAN)
# ==========================================
# Behavioral taxonomy via HDBSCAN density clustering. Separates the dominant
# botnet mass from human/agentic anomalies using density-based geometry —
# HDBSCAN natively ejects outliers as noise (-1), surfacing the sessions that
# matter most for forensic review.
#
# Inputs:  features_df  (Phase III-A)
# Outputs: features_df  enriched with cluster_id + predicted_label
#          cluster_mapping, LABEL_* constants, HUMAN_LABELS, TAXONOMY_PALETTE

import logging
import warnings
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple

import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.cluster import HDBSCAN
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.preprocessing import MaxAbsScaler, RobustScaler

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore', category=RuntimeWarning)
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ─── TAXONOMY CONSTANTS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Single source of truth — referenced by Phases V, VIII, X, XI, XIII.

LABEL_ECHO_SWARM    = 'Commodity Botnet (Echo Swarm)'
LABEL_SLOW_SCANNER  = 'Automated Scanner (Slow-Cycle)'
LABEL_SCRAPER_AGENT = 'Targeted Scraper / AI Agent'
LABEL_BULK_EXFIL    = 'Automated Scraper (Bulk Exfiltration)'
LABEL_HUMAN_VOYEUR  = 'Human Voyeur / Interactive Threat'
LABEL_HUMAN_GENERAL = 'Human (General Probing)'
LABEL_APT_HYBRID    = 'Advanced Persistent Threat (Hybrid Manual + Tooling)'
LABEL_NOISE         = 'Asymmetric Anomaly (HDBSCAN Noise)'

HUMAN_LABELS: List[str] = [LABEL_HUMAN_VOYEUR, LABEL_HUMAN_GENERAL, LABEL_APT_HYBRID]

TAXONOMY_PALETTE: Dict[str, str] = {
    LABEL_ECHO_SWARM:    '#d62728',
    LABEL_SLOW_SCANNER:  '#ff7f0e',
    LABEL_SCRAPER_AGENT: '#9467bd',
    LABEL_BULK_EXFIL:    '#8c564b',
    LABEL_HUMAN_VOYEUR:  '#2ca02c',
    LABEL_HUMAN_GENERAL: '#17becf',
    LABEL_APT_HYBRID:    '#e377c2',
    LABEL_NOISE:         '#7f7f7f',
}

# Feature column partitioning — mirrors Phase III-A.
# avg_keystroke_speed excluded: it is a named alias for mean_delta
# and including both would double-weight inter-command timing.
TEMPORAL_FEATURE_COLS: List[str] = [
    'mean_delta', 'entropy_delta', 'burst_ratio',
    'biological_variance', 'command_complexity', 'unique_commands',
]
SPARSE_FEATURE_COLS: List[str] = [
    'command_rarity_score', 'lure_file_hits', 'lure_password_hits',
    'deep_trap_hits', 'bulk_exfil_score', 'friction_bypassed',
]
NON_FEATURE_COLS = {
    'session_id', 'source', 'anomaly_score', 'predicted_label',
    'cluster_id', 'avg_keystroke_speed',
}


# ─── PHASE III-B-1: TAXONOMY ASSIGNMENT ────────────────────────────────────────

def assign_taxonomy_label(features: pd.Series, is_dense_core: bool = False) -> str:
    """
    Two-path taxonomy assignment:

    Dense core (is_dense_core=True): centroid burst_ratio splits Echo Swarm
    (>=0.9, machine-speed) from Slow-Cycle Scanner (<0.9, throttled).

    Noise evaluation (is_dense_core=False): weighted scoring matrix across all
    behavioral signals. Lure signals carry the highest weight — file-content
    interaction requires comprehension beyond script capability.
    """
    burst   = float(features.get('burst_ratio',          0.0))
    ent     = float(features.get('entropy_delta',         0.0))
    bio_var = float(features.get('biological_variance',   1.0))
    rarity  = float(features.get('command_rarity_score',  0.0))
    exfil   = float(features.get('bulk_exfil_score',      0.0))
    fric    = float(features.get('friction_bypassed',     0.0))
    lure_f  = float(features.get('lure_file_hits',        0.0))
    lure_pw = float(features.get('lure_password_hits',    0.0))
    deep    = float(features.get('deep_trap_hits',        0.0))

    if is_dense_core:
        return LABEL_ECHO_SWARM if burst >= 0.9 else LABEL_SLOW_SCANNER

    scores = {
        LABEL_ECHO_SWARM:    0.5,
        LABEL_SCRAPER_AGENT: 0.0,
        LABEL_BULK_EXFIL:    0.0,
        LABEL_HUMAN_VOYEUR:  0.0,
        LABEL_HUMAN_GENERAL: 0.0,
        LABEL_APT_HYBRID:    0.0,
    }

    if lure_pw > 0:
        scores[LABEL_SCRAPER_AGENT] += (lure_pw * 2.5) + burst
    if exfil > 0:
        scores[LABEL_BULK_EXFIL]    += (exfil * 2.0) + (burst * 2.0)
    if (lure_f + deep + fric) > 0:
        scores[LABEL_HUMAN_VOYEUR]  += ((lure_f + deep + fric) * 3.0) + (bio_var * 0.15)
    if bio_var > 3.0 or ent > 0.1:
        scores[LABEL_HUMAN_GENERAL] += (bio_var * 0.2) + (ent * 10.0) + (rarity * 0.05)
    if bio_var > 10.0 and (exfil > 0 or burst > 0.85):
        scores[LABEL_APT_HYBRID]    += (bio_var * 0.3) + (exfil * 2.0) + (rarity * 0.1)

    return max(scores, key=scores.get)


# ─── PHASE III-B-2: CLUSTERING ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

def apply_density_clustering(
    features_df: pd.DataFrame,
    min_cluster_size: int = 15,
) -> Tuple[pd.DataFrame, Dict[int, str]]:
    """
    HDBSCAN clustering with centroid-derived taxonomy assignment.

    Why HDBSCAN: handles severe class imbalance (>99% botnet mass vs <0.02%
    human sessions) without forcing equal-density spherical clusters. Natively
    ejects outliers as noise (-1) — the primary mechanism for surfacing
    human/agentic candidates for forensic review.

    Taxonomy flow:
      1. Cluster centroids (medians) → assign_taxonomy_label(is_dense_core=True)
      2. Noise sessions (-1) → assign_taxonomy_label(is_dense_core=False)
    """
    cluster_labels: Dict[int, str] = {}
    results_df = features_df.copy()

    # Fill NaN with behaviorally appropriate defaults before clustering.
    # Single-command sessions (Echo Swarm) have no deltas → burst=1.0, variance=1.0.
    results_df = results_df.fillna({
        'mean_delta': 0.0, 'entropy_delta': 0.0,
        'burst_ratio': 1.0, 'biological_variance': 1.0,
    }).fillna(0)

    if len(results_df) < min_cluster_size:
        logger.warning('Insufficient data (%d sessions, min %d).', len(results_df), min_cluster_size)
        results_df['predicted_label'] = results_df.get('predicted_label', LABEL_NOISE)
        return results_df, cluster_labels

    active_temporal = [c for c in TEMPORAL_FEATURE_COLS if c in results_df.columns]
    active_sparse   = [c for c in SPARSE_FEATURE_COLS   if c in results_df.columns]
    feature_cols    = active_temporal + active_sparse

    preprocessor = ColumnTransformer([
        ('temporal', RobustScaler(),  active_temporal),
        ('sparse',   MaxAbsScaler(), active_sparse),
    ], remainder='drop')
    X = preprocessor.fit_transform(results_df[feature_cols])

    clusterer = HDBSCAN(min_cluster_size=min_cluster_size, n_jobs=-1)
    results_df['cluster_id'] = clusterer.fit_predict(X)

    # Centroid-derived labels for dense cores
    for cid in sorted(c for c in results_df['cluster_id'].unique() if c != -1):
        centroid = results_df[results_df['cluster_id'] == cid][feature_cols].median()
        cluster_labels[cid] = assign_taxonomy_label(centroid, is_dense_core=True)

    # Per-session label: cluster label or noise scoring
    def _label(row: pd.Series) -> str:
        return (cluster_labels[int(row['cluster_id'])]
                if int(row['cluster_id']) != -1
                else assign_taxonomy_label(row[feature_cols], is_dense_core=False))

    results_df['predicted_label'] = results_df.apply(_label, axis=1)
    cluster_labels[-1] = LABEL_NOISE
    return results_df, cluster_labels


# ─── PHASE III-B-3: INTELLIGENCE QUADRANT VISUALIZATION ────────────────────────

def plot_intelligence_quadrant(features_df: pd.DataFrame) -> None:
    """
    Scatter plot of burst_ratio (x) vs biological_variance (y).

    Quadrants:
      Bottom-right (high burst, low variance) → Echo Swarm botnet mass
      Bottom-left  (low burst, low variance)  → Slow-Cycle scanners
      Top-left     (low burst, high variance) → Human general probing
      Top-right    (high burst, high variance)→ APT Hybrid
    """
    if features_df.empty or 'predicted_label' not in features_df.columns:
        return

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 9))

    for label, color in TAXONOMY_PALETTE.items():
        subset = features_df[features_df['predicted_label'] == label]
        if subset.empty:
            continue
        sizes = np.clip(subset.get('unique_commands', pd.Series(1)) * 8, 8, 200)
        ax.scatter(
            subset['burst_ratio'],
            subset.get('biological_variance', pd.Series(1.0, index=subset.index)),
            c=color, s=sizes, alpha=0.55, linewidths=0.3, edgecolors='white',
            label=f'{label}  (n={len(subset):,})',
        )

    ax.axvline(x=0.9, color='gray', linestyle='--', linewidth=1.0, alpha=0.5)
    ax.axhline(y=3.0, color='gray', linestyle='--', linewidth=1.0, alpha=0.5)

    for (x, y, txt) in [
        (0.02, 0.97, 'Human\nGeneral Probing'),
        (0.72, 0.97, 'APT Hybrid\n(Manual + Tooling)'),
        (0.02, 0.05, 'Slow-Cycle\nScanner'),
        (0.72, 0.05, 'Echo Swarm\n(Botnet Mass)'),
    ]:
        ax.text(x, y, txt, transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=8,
                color='gray', va='top' if y > 0.5 else 'bottom', style='italic')

    ax.set_xlabel('Burst Ratio  (0 = human-speed  →  1 = machine-speed)', fontsize=11)
    ax.set_ylabel('Biological Variance  (1 = robotic  →  high = human timing)', fontsize=11)
    ax.set_title(
        'Phase III-B: Intelligence Quadrant — Behavioral Separation Matrix\n'
        'Point size \u221d unique command count  |  Dashed lines = quadrant boundaries',
        fontsize=13, fontweight='bold',
    )
    ax.set_xlim(-0.05, 1.05)
    ax.set_yscale('log')
    ax.set_ylim(bottom=0.5)
    ax.legend(loc='center left', bbox_to_anchor=(1.01, 0.5),
              fontsize=9, framealpha=0.9, title='Behavioral Taxonomy', title_fontsize=10)
    ax.grid(True, linestyle=':', alpha=0.4)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


# ─── PHASE III-B-4: SUMMARY ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def print_taxonomy_summary(
    results_df: pd.DataFrame,
    cluster_labels: Dict[int, str],
) -> None:
    if results_df.empty:
        return

    total        = len(results_df)
    noise_count  = int((results_df['cluster_id'] == -1).sum())
    dense_count  = sum(1 for k in cluster_labels if k != -1)
    label_counts = results_df['predicted_label'].value_counts()
    human_count  = sum(label_counts.get(l, 0) for l in HUMAN_LABELS)

    print('\n' + '=' * 90)
    print('### PHASE III-B: HDBSCAN TAXONOMY COMPLETE ###')
    print('=' * 90)
    print(f'  Dense clusters identified:  {dense_count}')
    print(f'  Noise (outlier) sessions:   {noise_count:,}  — primary forensic candidate pool')
    print(f'  Human/agentic sessions:     {human_count:,}  across {len(HUMAN_LABELS)} taxonomy categories')
    if 'source' in results_df.columns:
        for src in ['LP1', 'LP2']:
            s = results_df[results_df['source'] == src]
            if not s.empty:
                h = sum(label_counts.reindex(HUMAN_LABELS, fill_value=0))
                print(f'    {src}: {len(s):,} sessions total')
    print('-' * 90)
    print('  CLUSTER CORE MAPPINGS (centroid-derived):')
    for cid, label in sorted((k, v) for k, v in cluster_labels.items() if k != -1):
        size   = int((results_df['cluster_id'] == cid).sum())
        burst  = results_df[results_df['cluster_id'] == cid]['burst_ratio'].median()
        print(f'    Cluster {cid:>2}  ({size:>6,} sessions)  →  {label:<45}  [burst: {burst:.3f}]')
    print('-' * 90)
    print('  BEHAVIORAL COMPOSITION:')
    for label, count in label_counts.items():
        marker = '  \u25c4 HUMAN/AGENTIC' if label in HUMAN_LABELS else ''
        print(f'    {count/total*100:>6.2f}%  |  {count:>6,}  |  {label}{marker}')
    print('-' * 90)
    print('  HDBSCAN selected over K-Means: handles severe class imbalance without')
    print('  forcing equal-density clusters. Noise ejection surfaces human candidates.')
    print('  Taxonomy is centroid-derived — ML drives attribution, not hard-coded rules.')
    print('=' * 90 + '\n')


# ─── EXECUTION ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

if __name__ == '__main__':
    try:
        features_df, cluster_mapping = apply_density_clustering(features_df)
        plot_intelligence_quadrant(features_df)
        print_taxonomy_summary(features_df, cluster_mapping)
    except NameError as e:
        logger.error('Missing upstream dependency: %s — ensure Phase III-A ran.', e)
